zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Rode於1253年出生在英國爾諾斯 洛德時，她的父親William de Rode23歲，母親Eva Watton19歲。她於大約1280年與John Davenport在英國爾奧德 洛德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda de Rode于1253年出生在英国爾諾斯 洛德时，她的父亲William de Rode23岁，母亲Eva Watton19岁。她于大约1280年与John Davenport在英国爾奧德 洛德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rode
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Ewald, Hans, Heinz, Oskar, Viktor, Claus, Frieda, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst.
German and French: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the first element hrōd ‘renown’. Compare Robert , Rudiger .

North German, Danish, and English: topographic name for someone who lived on land cleared for cultivation or in a clearing in woodland, from Middle Low German rode, Danish rothe, Old English rod. There are a number of places in England named with this word, for example Rode in Cheshire, and the surname may derive from any of these. Compare Rhode .

variants.name: Ruth
meanings: German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names formed with hrōd ‘renown’ (see Rode ).
(Rüth): habitational name from a place so named near Euskirchen in Rhineland.

English: from Middle English reuth(e) ‘pity’, a nickname perhaps for a charitable person or for a pitiable one. The personal name Ruth was little used in England in the Middle Ages among non-Jews, and is unlikely to have had any influence on the surname.

variants.name: Rody
meanings: German: variant of   Rode   1.
Scottish: variant of Rodie, from a pet form of Roderick .

variants.name: Robert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Rodeman
meanings: Americanized form of German Rodemann:
topographic name for someone who lived on woodland cleared for cultivation, a variant of Rode 2 with the addition of Middle Low German man ‘man’.
from an ancient Germanic personal name, Hrodman, formed with hrōd ‘renown, fame’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Winterroth
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived on the north side of a clearing (see   Rode  ).

variants.name: Roth
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a person with red hair, from Middle High German rōt, German rot ‘red’. As a Jewish name it is at least in part artificial: its frequency as a Jewish surname is disproportionate to the number of Jews who, one may reasonably assume, were red-headed during the period of surname adoption. This form of the German surname (especially in this sense and in the sense 2 below) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), where it is most common, and in some other European countries, e.g. Czechia and Croatia.

German and English (Middlesex): topographic name for someone who lived on land that had been cleared, from Old High German rod, Middle English roth(e) (Old English roth) ‘clearing’. In England, the name may also be a habitational name from any of the places like Rothend in Ashdon (Essex), Roe End in Markyate (Hertfordshire), Roe Green in Hatfield (Hertfordshire), or Roe Green in Sandon (Hertfordshire).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the first element hrōd ‘renown’. Compare Rode 1, Ross 4.

variants.name: Rohde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Hans, Heino, Heinz, Ewald, Gerhard, Helmut, Hermann, Klaus.
German and Danish: variant of   Rode   2 and a habitational name from any of various places called Rohde or Rohden in Lower Saxony, Saxony, Westphalia, and Hesse. Compare   Rhode   and   Rhody  .

variants.name: Roady
meanings: Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Rode  .

variants.name: Rodich
meanings: Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian Rodić: patronymic from a short form of a Slavic compound personal name based on the element rod ‘family, relatives’, e.g. Rodoslav.
Americanized form of Slovenian Rodič: surname probably related to (or derived from) Rode . Alternatively, it may be of the same origin as the Serbian and Croatian name above.
German: perhaps a variant of Rohdich, from a personal name based on ancient Germanic hrōd ‘renown, victory’.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Rode was born in 1253, in North Rode, Cheshire, England, her father, William de Rode, was 23 and her mother, Eva Watton, was 19. She married John Davenport about 1280, in Odd Rode, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Rode est née en 1253, à North Rode, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Rode, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Eva Watton, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé John Davenport environ 1280, à Odd Rode, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de Rode 1253 in North Rode, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Rode 23 und ihre Mutter Eva Watton 19. Sie hat um 1280 in Odd Rode, Cheshire, England John Davenport geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Rode è nata il nel 1253 nel North Rode, Cheshire, England, sua padre, William de Rode, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Eva Watton, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato John Davenport circa 1280, nel Odd Rode, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda de Rodeが1253年にイギリスチェシュアイアノース ロドで生まれた時，父William de Rodeは23歳，母Eva Wattonは19歳であった。約1280年にイギリスチェシュアイアオッド ロドでJohn Davenportと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Rode(이)가 1253년 영국 체실르 노르스 로드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Rode(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eva Watton(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1280년 영국 체실르 오드 로드에서 John Davenport(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Rode nasceu em 1253, em North Rode, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Rode, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Eva Watton, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com John Davenport aproximadamente 1280, em Odd Rode, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Rode родилась в 1253 г. в местности норт род, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Rode было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Eva Watton, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Davenport около 1280 г. в местности одд род, чешир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda de Rode nació en 1253, en North Rode, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Rode, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Eva Watton, tenía 19 años. Se casó con John Davenport aproximadamente 1280, en Odd Rode, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
